item.title: Web dashboard security gaps — path traversal, missing CSRF, wrong HTTP status codes, no rate limiting
item.intent: Harden the web dashboard against common web attacks. Path traversal must be airtight, state-changing endpoints must have CSRF protection, errors must return correct HTTP status codes, and resource-intensive endpoints must be rate-limited.
item.description: Code review found four issues in src/web/server.tsx: (1) Path traversal check uses startsWith which passes /home/user-evil for /home/user. (2) POST endpoints for deploy/restart/shutdown/init have no CSRF token validation. (3) Error responses return HTTP 200 instead of 4xx/5xx. (4) /api/logs endpoint accepts unbounded line count with no rate limiting.
steps: Fix path traversal: use path.relative() or check resolved path has / after home dir
Add CSRF token generation and validation middleware for all POST routes
Return proper HTTP status codes (500 for server errors, 400 for bad input)
Cap /api/logs line count at a reasonable maximum and add rate limiting
Add tests for path traversal edge cases and CSRF rejection
acceptanceCriteria: Path traversal check uses path.relative() or validates next char is /
All POST endpoints validate a CSRF token
Error responses return appropriate HTTP 4xx/5xx status codes
/api/logs endpoint has a maximum line count (e.g. 10000) and basic rate limiting
